response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend developer with a diverse portfolio, but he does not meet the most critical hard requirements for this role. Most disqualifying is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a mandatory qualification. Beyond that, his background is overwhelmingly in web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js) with only superficial and dated C/C++ exposure; there is no evidence of Linux kernel-level work, embedded firmware development, or any experience with the required networking security protocols such as MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, or L2/L3 switch architectures.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Demonstrated low-latency and systems-adjacent work at Sociomantic, indicating some performance-aware engineering mindset
Exposure to Linux-based infrastructure and DevOps tooling, including Docker and CI/CD pipelines
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — this is an explicit, non-negotiable hard requirement for the role
No demonstrable experience with embedded networking security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, L2/L3 switching)
C programming experience is minimal and dated (brief NI stint in 2014–2015); the role demands strong, sustained C proficiency for UNIX/embedded development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol experience
Long software engineering career with systems exposure
